boogie/stores/columnar

Imports

../wal

Types

AggKind = enum
  akCount, akSum, akMin, akMax, akAvg
AggregateSpec = object
  column*: string
  kind*: AggKind
  alias*: string
ColumnarError = object of CatchableError
ColumnarStore = object
  rootDir*: string
  wal*: Wal
  tables*: Table[string, ColumnarTable]
  walFlushEveryOps*: int
  pendingOps*: int
ColumnSchema = object
  name*: string
  kind*: ColumnType
  nullable*: bool
  codec*: CompressionCodec
Schema for a single column, including its name, type, nullability, and compression codec. In a production system, you would likely want to include additional metadata such as encoding parameters, statistics for query optimization, and support for more complex types. 
ColumnType = enum
  ctInt64, ctFloat64, ctBool, ctString, ctJson

A simple set of column types.

Adding more complex types (e.g. nested structures, arrays) is reserved for future work, but the current design should be flexible enough to accommodate them with some adjustments to the normalization and storage logic.

CompressionCodec = enum
  ccNone, ccRle, ccDictionary
Filter = object
  column*: string
  op*: FilterOp
  value*: JsonNode
  values*: seq[JsonNode]
FilterOp = enum
  foEq, foNe, foGt, foGte, foLt, foLte, foIn
JoinType = enum
  jtInner, jtLeft, jtRight, jtFull, jtCross
TableSchema = object
  name*: string
  primaryKey*: string
  columns*: seq[ColumnSchema]
  rowCount*: int64

Procs

proc aggregate(s: ColumnarStore; table: string; specs: seq[AggregateSpec];
               filters: seq[Filter] = @[]): JsonNode {....raises: [ColumnarError,
    KeyError, IOError, OSError, JsonParsingError, ValueError],
    tags: [ReadDirEffect, ReadIOEffect, WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
proc checkpoint(s: var ColumnarStore) {....raises: [IOError, WalError],
                                        tags: [WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
proc createTable(s: var ColumnarStore; schema: TableSchema; sync: bool = true) {.
    ...raises: [ColumnarError, IOError, WalError, KeyError, OSError],
    tags: [TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadDirEffect, WriteDirEffect],
    forbids: [].}
proc dropTable(s: var ColumnarStore; table: string; sync: bool = true) {.
    ...raises: [ColumnarError, IOError, WalError, OSError],
    tags: [TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadDirEffect],
    forbids: [].}
proc insertBatch(s: var ColumnarStore; table: string; rows: seq[JsonNode];
                 sync: bool = true) {....raises: [ColumnarError, KeyError, IOError,
    WalError, OSError], tags: [TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadDirEffect,
                               WriteDirEffect], forbids: [].}
proc join(s: ColumnarStore; joinType: JoinType; leftTable, rightTable: string): seq[
    JsonNode] {....raises: [ColumnarError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
TODO - Implement join logic for inner, left, right, full, and cross joins. This will likely involve scanning both tables, building hash maps for join keys, and producing combined rows according to the join type 
proc openColumnarStore(rootDir: string; walPath: string = "";
                       walFlushEveryOps: int = 100): ColumnarStore {....raises: [
    OSError, IOError, WalError, ColumnarError, KeyError, JsonParsingError,
    ValueError], tags: [ReadDirEffect, WriteDirEffect, WriteIOEffect,
                        ReadIOEffect, TimeEffect], forbids: [].}
Open or create a columnar store at the given root directory. If walPath is provided, use that for the WAL file; otherwise, use a default path under rootDir. The walFlushEveryOps parameter controls how many operations can be buffered in memory before an automatic flush to disk is triggered. 
proc scan(s: ColumnarStore; table: string; projectedColumns: seq[string];
          filters: seq[Filter] = @[]; limit: int = 0): seq[JsonNode] {....raises: [
    ColumnarError, KeyError, IOError, OSError, JsonParsingError, ValueError],
    tags: [ReadDirEffect, ReadIOEffect, WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
Scan the specified table, applying optional filters and projecting only the requested columns.

Made with Nim. Generated: 2026-05-06 15:58:10 UTC
